memory.summary: I have a free afternoon, but my term project is barely started and due Thursday. Relaxing now feels like avoiding the hard work ahead.

desires: Complete the term project before Thursday so I don't have to face the panic of a last-minute crash.
Avoid looking incompetent or unreliable to my professor and cohort by delivering something tangible, even if it's just a partial draft.
Push through this zero-energy state immediately because sitting in stillness feels like a trap that will only deepen the debt tomorrow.

goals.description: Execute a strict 45-minute isolated drafting block on the term project document with all notifications silenced to ensure no trace of my two-day stall is visible.
goals.rationale: My primary value system weights academic outcomes at 0.5, making the potential reputational damage of admitting to the cohort_3 that I've been stalling catastrophic. While my avoidance tendency (conflict: avoidant) makes this anxiety-inducing, my deliberate planning horizon dictates that securing this 'tangible start' is the only way to rebuild credibility without triggering a full-blown avoidance spiral. This minimal action creates the buffer needed to avoid shame later.

goals.description: Complete the trivial easy chore immediately after the drafting block ends to secure an instant win and restore baseline reliability.
goals.rationale: Maintaining social trust with teammate_A and general reliability matters for my long-term network health, but it does not justify sacrificing the high-stakes buffer required for the term project. Engaging with low-yield tasks now would trigger fear-based avoidance rather than productive completion. I will handle this post-project to ensure I don't waste mental energy when total focus demands are required.

agent.last_reasoning: This secures the high-priority 'tangible start' for the term project, protecting my academic reputation and avoiding shame spirals. I will keep notifications silenced to ensure no trace of my stall is visible, weighing this against social friction to prioritize long-term credibility.
